issues.title: reentrancy-events
issues.description:
Reentrancy in Crowdfunding.withdrawFunds() (contract.sol#79-88):
	External calls:
	- (success,None) = owner.call{value: amount}() (contract.sol#84)
	Event emitted after the call(s):
	- FundsWithdrawn(owner,amount) (contract.sol#87)
issues.recommendation: Review and apply security best practices

issues.title: reentrancy-events
issues.description:
Reentrancy in Crowdfunding.refund() (contract.sol#91-102):
	External calls:
	- (success,None) = msg.sender.call{value: contributedAmount}() (contract.sol#98)
	Event emitted after the call(s):
	- RefundIssued(msg.sender,contributedAmount) (contract.sol#101)
issues.recommendation: Review and apply security best practices

issues.title: timestamp
issues.description:
Crowdfunding.refund() (contract.sol#91-102) uses timestamp for comparisons
	Dangerous comparisons:
	- block.timestamp <= deadline (contract.sol#92)
issues.recommendation: Review and apply security best practices

issues.title: timestamp
issues.description:
Crowdfunding.contribute(uint256) (contract.sol#58-70) uses timestamp for comparisons
	Dangerous comparisons:
	- block.timestamp > deadline (contract.sol#59)
issues.recommendation: Review and apply security best practices

issues.title: solc-version
issues.description:
Version constraint ^0.8.20 contains known severe issues (https://solidity.readthedocs.io/en/latest/bugs.html)
	- VerbatimInvalidDeduplication
	- FullInlinerNonExpressionSplitArgumentEvaluationOrder
	- MissingSideEffectsOnSelectorAccess.
It is used by:
	- ^0.8.20 (contract.sol#2)

issues.title: Dependence on predictable environment variable (SWC 116)
issues.description:
A control flow decision is made based on The block.timestamp environment variable.
The block.timestamp environment variable is used to determine a control flow decision. Note that the values of variables like coinbase, gaslimit, block number and timestamp are predictable and can be manipulated by a malicious miner. Also keep in mind that attackers know hashes of earlier blocks. Don't use any of those environment variables as sources of randomness and be aware that use of these variables introduces a certain level of trust into miners.
Classification: SWC-116

issues.title: State access after external call (SWC 107)
issues.description:
Read of persistent state following external call
The contract account state is accessed after an external call to a fixed address. To prevent reentrancy issues, consider accessing the state only before the call, especially if the callee is untrusted. Alternatively, a reentrancy lock can be used to prevent untrusted callees from re-entering the contract in an intermediate state.
Classification: SWC-107
issues.recommendation: Validate external call targets and use checks-effects-interactions

issues.title: Dependence on predictable environment variable (SWC 116)
issues.description:
A control flow decision is made based on The block.timestamp environment variable.
The block.timestamp environment variable is used to determine a control flow decision. Note that the values of variables like coinbase, gaslimit, block number and timestamp are predictable and can be manipulated by a malicious miner. Also keep in mind that attackers know hashes of earlier blocks. Don't use any of those environment variables as sources of randomness and be aware that use of these variables introduces a certain level of trust into miners.
Classification: SWC-116

issues.title: External Call To User-Supplied Address (SWC 107)
issues.description:
A call to a user-supplied address is executed.
An external message call to an address specified by the caller is executed. Note that the callee account might contain arbitrary code and could re-enter any function within this contract. Reentering the contract in an intermediate state may lead to unexpected behaviour. Make sure that no state modifications are executed after this call and/or reentrancy guards are in place.
Classification: SWC-107
issues.recommendation: Validate external call targets and use checks-effects-interactions

issues.title: Transaction Order Dependence (SWC 114)
issues.description:
The value of the call is dependent on balance or storage write
This can lead to race conditions. An attacker may be able to run a transaction after our transaction which can change the value of the call
Classification: SWC-114

stage2_metadata.security_summary:
Contract Type: Custom
Classification Confidence: 90%
Access Control: role_based
Security Features: Standard
Implementation: Custom from scratch
Subtype: crowdfunding

Reasoning: The contract involves managing contributions towards a funding goal, which is specific to crowdfunding and does not fit into any standard template like ERC20, ERC721, etc.

classification.reasoning: The contract involves managing contributions towards a funding goal, which is specific to crowdfunding and does not fit into any standard template like ERC20, ERC721, etc.
